meta.reportLabel: Sun - THE HORMUZ CAP BREAKS: the week-long tell (oil refusing to rise on escalation) reversed. IRGC declared the Strait 'COMPLETELY CLOSED' (3 ships/24h vs ~110), the US ran a 2nd strike wave Sat - Brent surged to ~$88 (+4.6% Fri), WTI back >$80, crude +14% on the week. The oil spike re-fires US inflation -> Sept-hike odds jumped to ~56% (from ~44%), DXY ~100.8: the soft-USD slide is OVER. JPY is the clean oil-loser at the MoF 162 line; gold recovered >$4,000. Short USD/CAD is neutralised (both oil-inflation winners); CAD/JPY LONG upgrades back to HIGH. USOIL LONG (MED). Watch a MoF strike at 162.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The Hormuz cap finally breaks and re-writes the board. Over the weekend the escalation went vertical - the IRGC declared the Strait 'completely closed' (just 3 ships transited in 24h vs ~110 normally) and the US ran a second strike wave Saturday - and oil finally broke its cap: Brent ~$88 (+4.6% Fri), WTI back above $80, crude +14% on the week. That single change cascades: the oil spike re-fires US inflation, so the Fed-stays-high trade re-armed (Sept-hike odds ~56% from ~44%, DXY ~100.8) and the soft-USD slide is over; the ~100%-importer JPY is the clean terms-of-trade loser pinned at the MoF 162 line; gold recovered above $4,000. Short USD/CAD is neutralised (both are oil-inflation winners now); the cleanest two-leg trade flips to CAD/JPY LONG (upgraded to HIGH).
meta.nextBigEvent: The Strait of Hormuz is the dominant driver: IRGC-declared 'completely closed', ~20% of world oil flow effectively halted, the US striking to protect shipping and the June 60-day MOU dead. A sudden Oman/ceasefire breakthrough crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium in minutes; further escalation re-spikes it. Then Mon Jul 21 NZ Q2 CPI (ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5%, above RBNZ's 3.9% call) is the NZD swing, with Jul 23 ECB and Jul 28-29 FOMC framing the back half of the month.

dailyRead: The thing that held the whole board in consolidation for a week - oil refusing to rise on escalation - finally gave way, and it re-writes the trade map. Over the weekend the escalation went vertical: the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely closed', only 3 ships transited in 24 hours (versus ~110 normally), and the US ran a second wave of strikes on Saturday after Iran killed American service members. Oil finally broke its cap - Brent surged to ~$88 (+4.6% Friday), WTI back above $80 to one-month highs, crude up >14% on the week. That single change cascades: the oil spike re-fires US inflation, so the Fed-stays-high trade re-armed - September-hike odds jumped to ~56% (from ~44%) and DXY firmed to ~100.8; the soft-USD slide is over. The ~100%-energy-importer JPY is the clean loser of a terms-of-trade shock, pinned near 162 at the MoF's intervention line. Gold recovered back above $4,000 (~$4,016). Net: short USD/CAD is neutralised (both are oil-inflation winners now, CAD as the petro-currency, USD via the Fed), while the cleanest two-leg trade flips to CAD/JPY LONG (upgraded to HIGH). Cleanest leans: CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH); USOIL LONG (MED) as the cap breaks; short the high-betas vs CAD (AUD/CAD, NZD/CAD SHORT, MED); gold RANGE->long tilt. The one landmine: USD/JPY at 162 is a live MoF-strike zone - size the yen-shorts for an intervention snap.

today.headline: Petro-currency finally gets its real oil tailwind - the Hormuz cap broke (Brent ~$88); CAD/JPY LONG upgrades to HIGH
today.read: The oil spike is exactly the scenario Macklem flagged ('hikes if oil spikes'), so CAD gets a genuine terms-of-trade boost as a net energy exporter. CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH) is the cleanest two-leg trade; short the high-betas vs CAD (AUD/CAD, NZD/CAD). Risk: a ceasefire crushes oil AND flips the BoC dovish; USD/CAD is now a two-way, not a short.

today.headline: The soft-USD slide is OVER - the oil spike re-fires inflation, Sept-hike odds jumped to ~56%, DXY firmed to ~100.8
today.read: Higher oil feeds straight into US inflation, re-arming the Fed-stays-high trade - a hawkish lean that is rising again into the Jul 28-29 FOMC. That neutralises short USD/CAD (both are oil-inflation winners now). Risk: a sudden oil de-escalation deflates the inflation scare and re-softens the USD; a soft payroll re-arms the slide.

today.headline: The clean oil-shock loser - a ~100% energy importer pinned near 162, testing the MoF's 162 intervention line
today.read: The oil spike is a direct terms-of-trade shock; USD/JPY has repeated failures at 163 and steady bids at 162, the MoF's line (the Y11.73tn April precedent unused). JPY is the weakest currency - CAD/JPY LONG the clean expression. Risk: a stealth or overt MoF strike snaps the yen higher fast and hits every XXX/JPY carry-long.

today.headline: Softer - the oil terms-of-trade hit + a firm USD both weigh; EUR/USD leans lower into Jul 23 ECB
today.read: As a large energy importer, the oil spike is a terms-of-trade drag, and the firmer dollar compounds it - EUR/USD leans lower. Jul 23 ECB is the next test. Risk: a hawkish ECB core tone (core 2.5%) hands it a leg; an oil de-escalation lifts the relief bid.

today.headline: No change - hot Q2 CPI Monday (ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5%, above RBNZ's 3.9%), but the oil-risk-off caps it
today.read: The swing is imminent and hot - a hot Jul 21 print re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike - but the oil-shock risk-off tail + a firmer USD cap the high-beta NZD. NZD/CAD SHORT holds on the oil leg. Risk: an oil-shock flush hits it; a sub-4% print; a ceasefire.

today.headline: No change - firm rate story (services 3.7%), but a high-beta passenger to the oil shock; UK June CPI ~Jul 22
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, but a firmer USD and an oil-shock risk-off tail cap the cable tailwind. GBP/USD RANGE->short tilt; GBP/JPY carry-long tilt holds. Risk: a risk-off flush; a firmer USD; a soft UK CPI (~Jul 22).

today.headline: No change - oil-shock risk-off + firm USD cap it on both sides; Jun jobs ~Jul 23
today.read: Sticky-core floor, but the oil-shock risk-off tail and the firmer USD cap it both ways. Thin Aug hike tail (~36%), China CPI +1%. Short vs CAD (AUD/CAD). Risk: a re-escalation flush re-softens it; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) + a ceasefire re-arm the hawks.

strength.verdict: STRONGEST — the petro-currency gets its real oil tailwind
strength.tag: oil spike live; USD/CAD now a two-way
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (gasoline-driven; ex-gas 2.2%, core ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5%); BoC held 2.25% (6th straight) - Macklem: hikes possible IF oil spikes. The oil spike (Brent ~$88) is exactly that scenario, so the conditional-hawkish hold now has live upside and CAD gets a genuine net-energy-exporter terms-of-trade boost.
strength.forward: 🦅 The oil spike gives the BoC's oil-conditional hold live upside and CAD a real terms-of-trade tailwind; CAD/JPY LONG upgrades to HIGH. But short USD/CAD is neutralised - both are oil-inflation winners now. Risk: a ceasefire crushes oil AND flips BoC dovish; a risk-off flush hits CAD's beta leg. Next BoC Sep (data-dependent).

strength.verdict: FIRMER — the oil-inflation channel re-arms the no-cut trade
strength.drivers: June CPI -0.4% m/m -> 3.5% y/y (from 4.2%); core FLAT m/m -> 2.6%. NFP +57k (Jun); claims 208k. Fed 3.50-3.75% hold. The oil spike feeds directly into US inflation, so the soft-USD slide is over: Sept-hike odds jumped to ~56% (from ~44%) and DXY firmed to ~100.8 into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Higher oil -> higher US inflation -> Fed-stays-high: a hawkish lean that is rising again. That neutralises short USD/CAD. Risk: a sudden oil de-escalation deflates the inflation scare and re-softens the USD; a soft payroll re-arms the slide. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.verdict: FIRM — own rate story, but a high-beta passenger to the oil shock
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). A firmer USD and an oil-shock risk-off tail cap the cable tailwind; GBP is a high-beta passenger.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30, but a firmer USD + risk-off cap the near-term tailwind. Risk: a risk-off flush; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the check; a firmer USD. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.verdict: FIRM — hot Q2 CPI Monday, but the oil-risk-off caps it
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, scripted dovish (headline seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2). But ANZ now sees Q2 CPI 4.4%, BNZ 4.5% (Jul 21) - ABOVE that call - a hawkish risk. The oil-shock risk-off tail + a firmer USD cap the high-beta NZD.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The Jul 21 Q2 CPI is the swing - a hot print (4.4-4.5%) re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike. Risk: an oil-shock flush hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike; a ceasefire. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.verdict: SOFTER — oil terms-of-trade hit + a firm USD weigh
strength.tag: energy importer; leans lower into ECB
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. As a large energy importer, the oil spike is a terms-of-trade drag, and the firmer dollar compounds it - EUR/USD leans lower.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Oil terms-of-trade hit + a firm USD = a lower lean into Jul 23 ECB. Risk: a hawkish ECB core tone (core 2.5%) hands EUR a leg; an oil de-escalation lifts the relief bid. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp ~4.3% loosening (June jobs ~Jul 23). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. Sticky-core floor, but the oil-shock risk-off tail and the firmer USD cap it on both sides.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Oil-shock risk-off + firm USD cap = two-way; short vs CAD. Risk: a re-escalation flush re-softens it; a ceasefire + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arm the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.verdict: WEAKEST — the oil-shock terms-of-trade loser at the MoF 162 line
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. As a ~100% energy importer, the oil spike is a direct terms-of-trade shock - USD/JPY is pinned near 162 with repeated 163 failures, testing the MoF's 162 line (Y11.73tn April precedent unused).
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 The oil-shock terms-of-trade hit pins the yen at the MoF line; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish is the only offset. Risk: a stealth or overt MoF strike snaps the yen higher fast; a genuine panic breaks the carry the other way. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.why: The clean oil-divergence, now a real two-leg move (upgraded from MED): the oil spike boosts net-exporter CAD (BoC oil-hawk live) and hammers the ~100%-importer JPY (pinned at the MoF 162 line).
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and CAD; a MoF strike snaps the yen higher fast; a broad risk-off flush hits CAD's beta leg.

symbols.why: The cap broke: WTI back >$80 (one-month highs), Brent ~$88, crude +14% on the week as the IRGC declared Hormuz 'completely closed' (3 ships/24h vs ~110) and the US ran a 2nd strike wave. Supply premium is now real.
symbols.risk: A sudden Oman/ceasefire breakthrough unwinds the premium in minutes; demand-destruction fear + OPEC+/Saudi spare capacity cap the top.

symbols.why: Oil-winner CAD vs an oil-shock-hit high-beta AUD (upgraded from LOW-MED): the spike lifts petro-CAD and the risk-off tail + firm USD cap AUD - the oil leg splits them cleanly.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and re-pins CAD; a hot AU Q2 CPI (late Jul) lifts AUD; a risk-on melt-up.

symbols.why: Oil-winner CAD vs a risk-off-capped NZD - the oil leg leads, though Monday's hot NZ Q2 CPI (4.4-4.5%) is a narrowing risk.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire; a hot NZ Q2 CPI Jul 21 lifts NZD; a risk-on melt-up.

symbols.why: The oil spike + firmer USD both push it up, but it is pinned at the MoF 162 intervention line with repeated 163 failures - the ceiling is policy, not price. A long tilt capped by the MoF.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike snaps it lower fast; an oil de-escalation caps the top; a soft-USD resumption.

symbols.why: The old star short is neutralised: both are oil-inflation winners now - CAD as the petro-currency, USD via the Fed-stays-high channel (Sept odds ~56%) - a two-way near ~1.38-1.40.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and flips BoC dovish (USD/CAD up); a soft US payroll re-softens the USD (USD/CAD down).

symbols.why: Recovered back above $4,000 (~$4,016 from ~$3,986) as the haven + inflation-hedge bid returned with the oil spike; only the firmer USD and higher real rates cap it. A long tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish FOMC repricing lifts real rates; a ceasefire deflates the geopolitics leg; a fresh escalation re-fires it.

symbols.why: Oil-importer EUR terms-of-trade hit + a firmer USD (Sept odds ~56%) - leans lower into Jul 23 ECB. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish ECB Jul 23; an oil de-escalation lifts the EUR relief bid.

symbols.why: Firm-rate GBP but a firmer USD + risk-off tail trim the cable - high-beta passenger. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish BoE read-through; an oil de-escalation softens the USD; UK CPI ~Jul 22.

symbols.why: Oil-loser JPY firms the carry, but the oil-shock risk-off + firm USD cap the high-beta AUD - offsetting. A mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike lifts JPY; an oil-shock flush hits AUD faster.

symbols.why: Weak-JPY carry + hot-Q2-CPI-risk NZD, but risk-off + firm USD cap the base. A mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock flush hits NZD; a sub-4% NZ CPI; a MoF strike.

symbols.why: Firm-rate GBP vs the oil-loser JPY at 162 - carry-long tilt holds, capped by the MoF line and risk-off.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike snaps JPY up; a risk-off flush hits GBP.

symbols.why: Both energy-importers hit by oil, but the yen is the weaker leg at the MoF line - mild carry-long tilt.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike; a hawkish ECB Jul 23; an oil re-spike hits both.

symbols.why: Sticky-core AUD vs a firmed USD + the oil-shock risk-off tail - the mild long tilt is capped. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire risk-on melt-up; a soft USD; Jun jobs ~Jul 23.

symbols.why: Hot-Q2-CPI-risk NZD vs a firmed USD + risk-off cap. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot NZ Q2 CPI Jul 21 lifts NZD; an oil de-escalation softens the USD.

symbols.why: Laggard oil-importer EUR vs an NZD with a confirmed hot-CPI hawkish risk but a risk-off cap - two-way.
symbols.risk: A sub-4% NZ CPI lifts EUR; a hawkish ECB Jul 23.

symbols.why: Both high-beta and oil-risk-capped, but NZD carries the hotter near-term CPI (Jul 21) - mild short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot AU Q2 CPI flips it; a sub-4% NZ CPI.

symbols.why: Two oil-hit currencies (importer EUR vs risk-off AUD) - no clean vector.
symbols.risk: AU Q2 CPI late July; a hawkish ECB Jul 23.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a doubly-capped AUD - mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot AU CPI; a risk-on melt-up lifts AUD; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs an NZD with a confirmed hot-CPI hawkish risk - two firm legs, edge thin.
symbols.risk: A hot NZ Q2 CPI Jul 21 lifts NZD; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Firmed to ~100.8 as the oil-inflation channel re-armed the no-cut trade (Sept-hike odds ~56% from ~44%); the soft-USD slide is over - a firmer bias into the FOMC. A long tilt.
symbols.risk: A sudden oil de-escalation deflates the inflation scare; a soft payroll re-arms the slide.

symbols.why: The yen is the oil-shock terms-of-trade loser, pinned at the MoF 162 line, so the basket leans lower - the opposite ticket to the XXX/JPY carry-longs.
symbols.risk: A MoF intervention strike hands JPY a fast bid and spikes the basket; a genuine risk-off panic.

symbols.why: The oil spike flips from tailwind to energy-cost headwind for the record-stretched (~25,800) melt-up, and a firmer USD + a still-hot war compound it - leans lower.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and re-fires the melt-up; a soft-USD resumption.

symbols.why: Silver firmed with gold's haven leg (~$57 from ~$56), but the oil-shock growth-fear caps the industrial-demand leg - a two-sided grind.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg hits industrial demand; a gold-led haven re-fire lifts it.

catalysts.note: Only 3 ships transited in 24h vs ~110 normally; US ran a 2nd strike wave Saturday after Iran killed US service members; ~20% of world oil flow effectively halted - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.

catalysts.event: Trump's 20% Hormuz toll + US naval blockade
catalysts.note: Blockade of Iranian ports in force; a demanded 20% toll on ALL Hormuz cargo - a structural supply-cost shock now the strait is shut.

Oman's traffic-management talks continued but the strait is shut and Iran's drones hit Oman; the June 60-day MOU is dead - a breakthrough crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium, a further escalation re-spikes it.
ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5% - ABOVE RBNZ's 'peaked 3.9%' call; a hot print re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike.
Hold; core-2.5% is the Sept-hike signal but the oil terms-of-trade hit clouds it.
Unemployment ~4.3% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Warsh round 2; hold; the oil-inflation spike firmed Sept-hike odds to ~56%.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162 at the MoF line; the oil bill is now a bigger drag.

geopolitics: The week-long tell - oil refusing to rise on escalation - finally reversed, and that is the single most important change on the board. Over the weekend the conflict escalated hard: the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz 'now highly unsafe and completely closed', and the choke is visible in the data - just 3 ships transited in the past 24 hours (one cargo, one tanker in, one tanker out) versus an average ~110 before the war. The US ran a second wave of strikes Saturday to 'swiftly punish' Iran after it killed American service members, aimed at degrading Iran's ability to threaten shipping; Iran's drones continued to target Gulf states including Oman. The June 60-day ceasefire MOU is effectively dead. With ~20% of the world's seaborne oil effectively halted, the premium the market had been capping for a week finally got paid: Brent surged to ~$88 (+4.6% Friday), WTI is back above $80 at one-month highs, crude is up >14% on the week, and US gasoline is +34% since the war began. FX transmission (Sunday - the board flips): (1) The USD firmed - the oil spike feeds straight into US inflation, re-arming the Fed-stays-high trade: Sept-hike odds jumped to ~56% (from ~44%) and DXY firmed to ~100.8. The soft-USD slide is over. (2) CAD, the net-energy-exporter, gets its real oil tailwind - the exact scenario Macklem flagged ('hikes if oil spikes') - but because the USD also firmed, short USD/CAD is neutralised (both are oil-inflation winners) and becomes a two-way. (3) The JPY is the clean loser - a ~100% energy importer taking a terms-of-trade shock, pinned near 162 at the MoF's intervention line, which makes CAD/JPY LONG the cleanest two-leg trade (upgraded to HIGH). (4) Gold recovered above $4,000 (~$4,016) on the haven + inflation-hedge bid, shifting to a RANGE->long tilt, capped by the firmer USD and real rates. (5) The high-betas (AUD, NZD) and the importer EUR are hit by the oil-shock risk-off tail + the firmer USD - short vs CAD. (6) GER40 flips from an oil tailwind to an energy-cost headwind. The one landmine cutting across every yen-short: USD/JPY at 162 is a live MoF-strike zone - an intervention would snap the yen higher in minutes and hit CAD/JPY and every XXX/JPY carry-long. Trade the oil-divergence, but keep the yen-short sizing intervention-aware.
